goal: Optimize the project by hardening the async import-task lifecycle without changing OCR rules or the visible polling states.
deliverable: A reusable task registry module with TTL-based pruning metadata
Router integration for upload parsing tasks
Governed requirement, plan, and runtime receipts
constraints: Keep the current upload and polling endpoints
Do not change OCR business logic
Preserve pending, processing, completed, and failed status semantics
acceptance_criteria: Task responses include lifecycle timestamps and expiry metadata
Terminal task state is pruned over time without dropping active tasks in normal use
Expired task lookups return a clear 404 detail
product_acceptance_criteria: Existing frontend polling keeps working with no required contract changes
Users see a clearer message when a task expires or disappears after restart
manual_spot_checks: Create a task and confirm the API still returns task_id
Poll a task and confirm status and result payload still match frontend expectations
Check that expired or missing tasks surface a clear error detail
completion_language_policy: Only claim this optimization slice complete after validation passes and the polling contract remains backward-compatible.
delivery_truth_contract: Report only verified changes, and call out any runtime behavior that was not exercised end-to-end in this session.
inferred_assumptions: The app is typically used by one operator at a time
The in-memory task model is acceptable if it is bounded and observable
Frontend code can ignore additional response fields safely
